title: pultenaea lanceolata
intro: Pultenaea lanceolata, also known as the Lance-leaf Bush Pea, is a small shrub native to Australia, belonging to the legume family and producing small yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds and skin conditions due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Pultenaea lanceolata include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and provide relief from skin irritations and infections. Pultenaea lanceolata is often prepared as a tincture or infusion for medicinal use, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
